query: Ukraine drone campaign Russian oil refineries strike rate June July 2026 - how many refinery strikes per month, is the campaign continuing to intensify, Moscow region drone attacks frequency
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings on Ukraine's drone campaign against Russian oil refineries in June–July 2026:

**Strike rate / base rate:** The most rigorous benchmark is **~26 refinery attacks across April–May 2026 combined (~13/month)** — matching the intensity that caused the Aug–Sep 2025 fuel shortages. "Over two dozen strikes since March" corroborates this pace, and June appears at or above it.

**Is it intensifying? Yes** — on every metric:
- More drones reaching targets than ever before (Carnegie)
- Reach extended to 2,000–2,500 km (Tyumen, Ufa in Western Siberia/Bashkortostan)
- 8 of Russia's top 10 refineries now hit; by end-May no European-Russia refinery had escaped
- ~25–28% (some sources ~one-third) of national refining capacity offline; crude processing down 12–13%
- 56 regions under fuel restrictions by July 1; Putin acknowledged the crisis and banned fuel exports

**Moscow-region attacks:** Occurred every few days in June with repeated airport closures — June 16, 18, 22, 25–26, and 30. The June 18 strike was the largest on Moscow in ~2 years (~180–200 drones, all four airports closed, 527 flights disrupted).

**Key caveat:** Figures blend Ukrainian claims, unverifiable Russian MoD intercept counts, and analyst estimates; "attacks launched" differs from "successful hits" (only ~10% of drones reach targets per Ukrainian sources). Full structured findings with 13 data points and 12 sources have been submitted.

key_facts: ~26 Ukrainian refinery attacks occurred across April–May 2026 combined (~13/month), matching the pace that caused Aug–Sep 2025 fuel shortages (Carnegie/Vakulenko via Meduza).
'Over two dozen strikes since March 2026' reported (Mint/WSJ, June 21).
8 of Russia's top 10 refineries have been struck; only Omsk and Angarsk (east of Urals) untouched as of June 29.
By end of May 2026, no major refinery in European Russia had escaped a Ukrainian drone attack (Reuters).
Campaign is intensifying: 'More Ukrainian drones are reaching their targets than ever before' (Carnegie); reach extended to 2,000–2,500 km (Tyumen, W. Siberia).
~25–28% of Russian refining capacity offline; some reports cite ~one-third after June 18 Moscow strike.
Crude processing dropped 12–13% (~650,000–700,000 bpd) in April–May vs. March baseline of 5.2 million bpd.
56 Russian regions had fuel restrictions by July 1, 2026 (up from ~53 mid-June); Putin acknowledged crisis and banned gasoline/jet fuel exports.
Moscow region attacked every few days in June 2026; June 18 was the largest strike on Moscow in ~2 years (~180–200 drones), all 4 airports closed, 527 flights disrupted.
Kapotnya (Moscow) refinery hit twice within one week (June 16 and June 18); repairs estimated 2 months to 2 years, possibly offline until 2027.
Russia claimed 660 drones downed June 25–26 (highest of the war) and 419 downed over 24hrs on June 30.
Ukrainian drone production scaling: Firepoint reportedly producing >100 FP-1 drones daily, ~10% reaching targets.

data_points.metric: Implied average refinery strikes per month (spring 2026 baseline)

Ukrainian drones downed nationwide over 24hrs June 30 (Russian claim)
